summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Shift4 promotes Global Blue execs
articles.description: The payments processor promoted three executives to leadership roles, naming Pier Francesco Nervini as head of its international division.

articles.title: Meta revamps ad payment policy
articles.description: The social media company is mandating that some of its advertisers make their monthly payments via invoice or debit starting next month.

articles.title: Par Tech faces investor pressure
articles.description: One of the point-of-sale software company’s largest shareholders began urging it to consider “strategic alternatives,” such as a sale, last week.

articles.title: AWS leaps into agentic payments
articles.description: Amazon Web Services, the tech giant’s cloud computing network, is gearing up for armies of AI-powered agents and transactions.

articles.title: Clarity Act stokes debate on crypto
articles.description: The bill aims to identify which agencies should regulate digital currencies.

articles.title: Boston Fed touts FedNow
articles.description: The Federal Reserve has been updating its instant payments service, FedNow, to attract more banks and ultimately reach more consumers.

articles.title: Pilot, NCR Voyix agree to 5-year pact
articles.description: The retailer, which has used NCR’s self-checkout kiosks for years, will now also deploy the payment service company’s point-of-sale technology.

articles.title: Affirm pursues affluent consumers
articles.description: Shoppers with more purchasing power spend more, and are more likely to use the company’s products again, an executive said.
